title: BRCA1 and BRCA2 hereditary breast and ovarian cancer susceptibility
interpretation: The record discusses inherited pathogenic variants in BRCA1 and BRCA2 and their association with hereditary breast and ovarian cancer risk.
fullText: BRCA1 c.68_69delAG is a frameshift pathogenic variant reported in hereditary breast and ovarian cancer. Heterozygous carriers show autosomal dominant cancer susceptibility. The paper discusses BRCA1 exon 2 variants and breast carcinoma, ovarian neoplasm, pancreatic cancer risk, family history, penetrance, and clinical surveillance.

title: Clinical interpretation of CFTR sequence variants
interpretation: The record summarizes CFTR variant interpretation with genotype and phenotype correlations relevant to cystic fibrosis.
fullText: CFTR c.1521_1523delCTT, p.Phe508del, is frequently reported in cystic fibrosis. Patients may be homozygous or compound heterozygous. The condition follows autosomal recessive inheritance and includes recurrent respiratory infections, pancreatic insufficiency, malabsorption, and variable severity.

title: SCN1A variants and developmental epileptic encephalopathy
interpretation: The record describes SCN1A variants in epilepsy and neurodevelopmental phenotypes.
fullText: SCN1A p.Arg1645 variants occur in the sodium channel ion transport domain. Affected patients have seizures, developmental delay, hypotonia, and variable intellectual disability. Many cases are heterozygous de novo variants, although inherited autosomal dominant familial epilepsy can be observed.

title: DMD dystrophinopathy genotype and phenotype correlations
interpretation: The record discusses dystrophinopathy caused by DMD variants with muscle and cardiac involvement.
fullText: DMD c.31+1G>A and frameshift variants can cause dystrophinopathy. Males are often hemizygous and the disorder follows X-linked recessive inheritance. Phenotypes include progressive muscular dystrophy, skeletal muscle atrophy, and dilated cardiomyopathy.
